VAL-CORE-001.evidence: 1) scene_state.rs contains `update_clock(&mut self, clock_text: &[u8])` method (line ~195) that: calls copy_front_to_back(), reads clock node's Content::Text to find glyph DataRef, builds new glyphs via bytes_to_shaped_glyphs, overwrites in-place via update_data (same-length), updates only content_hash on N_CLOCK_TEXT, calls mark_changed(N_CLOCK_TEXT), and swaps. No other node is touched or marked changed. 2) Unit test `incremental_clock_update_changes_only_clock` (scene.rs:2662) explicitly labeled VAL-CORE-001: builds full editor scene, snapshots all 8 nodes, performs incremental clock update (copy_front_to_back + in-place glyph overwrite + mark_changed(N_CLOCK_TEXT) + swap), then byte-compares every non-clock node against pre-update snapshot asserting identity, verifies clock content_hash changed, and confirms change list contains exactly [N_CLOCK_TEXT]. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass including this one.

VAL-CORE-002.evidence: 1) scene_state.rs contains `update_cursor(&mut self, cursor_pos, doc_text, chars_per_line, char_width, line_height, scroll_px)` method (line ~234) that: calls copy_front_to_back(), computes (line, col) via byte_to_line_col, updates only N_CURSOR x and y fields, calls mark_changed(N_CURSOR), and swaps. No other node is touched. 2) Unit test `incremental_cursor_update_changes_only_cursor` (scene.rs:2748) explicitly labeled VAL-CORE-002: builds full editor scene, snapshots all 8 nodes, performs incremental cursor move to position 5, then byte-compares every non-cursor node against pre-update snapshot asserting identity, verifies cursor x=40 (5*8) and y=0, verifies cursor width and background unchanged, and confirms change list contains exactly [N_CURSOR]. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass including this one.

VAL-CORE-003.evidence: 1) scene_state.rs contains `update_document_content(...)` method (line ~300) that: checks data buffer 75% threshold (falls back to full rebuild if exceeded), calls copy_front_to_back(), truncates to WELL_KNOWN_COUNT (removing old selection rects), re-layouts visible text lines, pushes new glyph data and text runs, updates N_DOC_TEXT content and content_hash, marks N_DOC_TEXT changed, updates N_CURSOR x/y position, marks N_CURSOR changed, then rebuilds selection rects if needed. Chrome nodes (N_ROOT, N_TITLE_BAR, N_TITLE_TEXT, N_CLOCK_TEXT, N_SHADOW, N_CONTENT) are never touched. 2) Unit test `incremental_doc_update_changes_doc_text_and_cursor` (scene.rs:3166) explicitly labeled VAL-CORE-003: builds scene with 'hello', does incremental update to 'hellox' (simulating character insert), verifies doc text content_hash changed, verifies cursor moved to x=48 (6*8), byte-compares all chrome nodes (ROOT, TITLE_BAR, TITLE_TEXT, CLOCK_TEXT, SHADOW, CONTENT) against pre-update snapshot asserting identity, and confirms change list contains exactly [N_DOC_TEXT, N_CURSOR]. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass including this one.

VAL-CORE-004.evidence: 1) scene_state.rs contains `update_selection(...)` method (line ~263) that: calls copy_front_to_back(), truncates to WELL_KNOWN_COUNT via set_node_count (removing old selection rects), updates cursor x/y/next_sibling, marks N_CURSOR changed, then calls allocate_selection_rects which allocates new nodes at indices 8+ as siblings of cursor, marks each new selection node changed. Chrome/doc text nodes are untouched. 2) Unit test `incremental_selection_manages_nodes` (scene.rs:2881) explicitly labeled VAL-CORE-004/VAL-CORE-010: builds editor scene, verifies 8 nodes, adds selection (alloc node at index 8, links as cursor sibling), verifies 9 nodes, clears selection (set_node_count(WELL_KNOWN_COUNT)), verifies back to 8 nodes. 3) Companion test `selection_cycle_no_node_leak` (scene.rs:2935) labeled VAL-CORE-010: cycles select/deselect 10 times with 2 rects each, asserts node count returns to 8 each cycle and new selection indices are always 8/9. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass including these.

VAL-CORE-005.evidence: 1) scene_state.rs `build_editor_scene(...)` method (line ~48) performs a full scene build: clears back buffer, pushes all glyph/text data, allocates 8 well-known nodes in order (0-7), sets up the complete tree structure (ROOT→TITLE_BAR→TITLE_TEXT+CLOCK_TEXT, ROOT→SHADOW→CONTENT→DOC_TEXT→CURSOR+selection_rects), and swaps. 2) The test helper `build_test_editor_scene` (scene.rs:2516) mirrors this exact structure: allocates 8 nodes, sets identical tree relationships (ROOT.first_child=TITLE_BAR, TITLE_BAR.first_child=TITLE_TEXT, TITLE_TEXT.next_sibling=CLOCK_TEXT, TITLE_BAR.next_sibling=SHADOW, SHADOW.next_sibling=CONTENT, CONTENT.first_child=DOC_TEXT, DOC_TEXT.first_child=CURSOR), with matching dimensions (1024x768, title_bar height 36, etc.). 3) Test `writer_build_typical_editor_scene` (scene.rs:565) verifies a scene build produces the expected tree structure (root→title→content→doc_text with correct node IDs and CLIPS_CHILDREN flag). 4) Test `incremental_cursor_matches_full_rebuild` (scene.rs:2811) labeled VAL-CORE-006 verifies that an incrementally-updated scene matches a fresh full build for cursor position — confirming full build produces correct baseline. 5) All incremental tests (VAL-CORE-001 through 004) start from build_test_editor_scene and verify the 8-node structure is intact after updates, implicitly validating the initial build is correct. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass with 0 failures.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-CORE-001: update_clock method and test verify only N_CLOCK_TEXT changes. VAL-CORE-002: update_cursor method and test verify only N_CURSOR x/y changes. VAL-CORE-003: update_document_content method and test verify N_DOC_TEXT+N_CURSOR change while chrome is unchanged. VAL-CORE-004: update_selection method and tests verify selection rect add/remove with correct node management. VAL-CORE-005: build_editor_scene produces correct 8-node tree structure validated by multiple tests including structure checks and incremental-vs-full-rebuild comparisons.
